verses.commentary: Children obeying parents in the Lord is right. Obedience is moral duty, not optional. 'In the Lord' grounds obedience in Christ's authority.
Honoring father and mother is the first command with promise. The fifth commandment applies to children. Promise accompanies this duty, showing its importance.
The promise is well-being and long life. Obedience brings blessing. God rewards honoring parents with prosperity and longevity.
Fathers must not exasperate children but bring them up in the Lord's training and instruction. Parental authority includes responsibility. Harsh treatment is forbidden; godly nurture is required.
Slaves obey earthly masters with respect, fear, and sincerity, as to Christ. Work is worship when done for Christ. Human authority represents divine authority.
Obedience is not just when watched but as slaves of Christ doing God's will. Integrity means consistent obedience. The real Master is Christ, not the human master.
Wholehearted service as to the Lord, not people. Motivation is divine, not human approval. Serving people is ultimately serving Christ.
The Lord rewards each for good done, slave or free. Divine justice is impartial. Earthly status does not affect heavenly reward.
Masters treat slaves well, without threats, knowing their Master is in heaven and shows no favoritism. Accountability to God moderates earthly authority. Partiality is absent in divine judgment.
Strength comes from the Lord and His mighty power. Human strength is insufficient. Divine power enables spiritual warfare.
Full armor of God enables standing against the devil's schemes. Warfare is defensive and requires divine equipment. Satan's tactics demand supernatural protection.
The struggle is not against flesh and blood but spiritual powers. Human enemies are not the real enemy. Cosmic evil requires spiritual weapons.
Full armor enables standing on the day of evil. Preparation is essential for survival. Standing, not advancing, is the goal in spiritual battle.
Standing firm requires truth as a belt and righteousness as a breastplate. Truth holds everything together; righteousness protects vital organs. These are foundational defenses.
Feet fitted with readiness from the gospel of peace. Stability and mobility come from peace. The gospel grounds and propels the warrior.
Faith is the shield extinguishing the evil one's flaming arrows. Faith defends against all attacks. Trust in God deflects Satan's assaults.
Salvation is the helmet, and the Spirit's sword is God's word. Assurance of salvation protects the mind; Scripture is the offensive weapon. Defense and offense combine in spiritual warfare.
Pray in the Spirit on all occasions with all kinds of prayers. Prayer is constant and varied. Alertness and perseverance characterize intercession for all saints.
Paul requests prayer for bold gospel proclamation. Even apostles need prayer support. Fearlessness in declaring the mystery is essential.
Paul is an ambassador in chains, needing courage to speak as he should. Imprisonment does not silence the gospel. Boldness is necessary even in adversity.
Tychicus will inform them of Paul's circumstances. Personal updates strengthen community bonds. Faithful servants carry news between separated believers.
Paul sends Tychicus specifically to inform and encourage. Communication serves encouragement. Knowing each other's situation fosters unity.
Peace and love with faith from God and Christ. These virtues come from divine source. Father and Son jointly bestow spiritual blessings.
Grace to all who love the Lord with undying love. Incorruptible love for Christ marks true believers. Grace accompanies genuine devotion.
